<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[ja:紀伊国造]][[simple:Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[ja:紀伊国造]][[simple:Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[ja:紀伊国造]][[simple:Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]




{{Expand Japanese}}

{{Infobox Japanese clan|founder={{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}} (mythical)<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />
Ōnagusahiko (historical) <ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />|image=File:Hinokuma Shrine.JPG|caption=Hinokuma Shrine, the traditional shrine of the family|parent house=[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]|image caption=Hinokuma Shrine, the traditional shrine of the family|alt=Hinokuma Shrine, the traditional shrine of the family|province of origin={{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}|home province={{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}}}

{{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀伊国造|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} is a [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] that had a uniquely high amount of power similar to the {{ill|Izumo clan|en|simple|User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}.<ref name="mw-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-03-10 |title=Kuni no miyatsuko {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8839 |access-date=2023-12-02 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310081317/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8839 |archive-date=2023-03-10 }}</ref>

The Kinoatai family was responsible for this office. According to the Sendai Kuji Hongi, a historic record, Amenomichine no Mikoto, a descendant of Kami Musubi no Mikoto, was the first to be appointed as Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of Emperor Jinmu. However, Ōnagusahiko is often considered the actual precursor of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀伊国造|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref name="mw-2023a" />

They run [[Hinokuma Shrine|Hinokuma Shrine]].<ref name="mw-2023a" /><ref name="web.archive.org-2023">{{Cite web|date=2023-12-16|title=Kinokuninomiyatsuko {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231216060822/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8597|access-date=2023-12-16|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> and are the Kuni no miyatsuko of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}.

They descend from {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}<ref name="web.archive.org-2023" />.

They are believed to be buried at {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|simple|User:Immanuelle/Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=43pp750404}}
{{in lang|ja}}</ref>..

They worshipped the daughters of Susanoo.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9161|access-date=2024-04-01|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
==Overview==
The Kinoatai family held a title called "Kuni no Miyatsuko" of the {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}. The title meant they were a provincial governor with both religious and secular duties. The title is mentioned in historic texts such as the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. The Chinese characters used to write the term in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] were adopted under the [[Ritsuryō system|Ritsuryō system]], a legal system in ancient Japan, and they can also be read as Kokuzo.<ref name="mw-2023a" />

The system of kuni no miyatsuko includes the chieftain of the Nagusa district, Nagusahiko, who was integrated into the imperial control structure in the early phases of [[kofun]] construction in the fifth century. Some theories suggest the kuni no miyatsuko system was established in the {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=sixth century|1=6th century}}.<ref name="mw-2023a" />

Following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reform|qq=}}, which aimed to consolidate imperial power and regulate land ownership in {{ill|645|en|645|qid=Q31040|lt=645|1=645}}, the kuni no miyatsuko also served as gunji, or district administrators. This dual role became less common after the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}.<ref name="mw-2023a" />

The {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀伊国造|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ran the [[Hinokuma Shrine|Hinokuma and Kunikakasu shrines]] and their current descendants serve as gūji, or chief priests. The Jōgan Gishiki, a document from the period, outlines the ritual for appointing the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀伊国造|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} who served these shrines.<ref name="mw-2023a" />

==Overview==

{{日本の氏族
|家名=紀伊国造
|家紋= 
|家紋|name=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|native_name= {{ruby|九枚笹|くまいささ}}<br/>{{ruby|熨斗|のし}}<br/>{{ruby|菊|きく}}
|本姓= 紀氏
|家祖= {{ill|Ame no Michine no Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Michine no Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940767}}
{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=1823}}
|種別= {{ill|Divine Clan|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Clan|1=Shinbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588768}}<br/>　{{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}<br/>　{{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|qid=Q241734|lt=Kazoku|1=Kazoku}}（{{ill|Baron|en|Baron|qid=Q165503|lt=Baron|1=Baron}}）
|出身地= {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}
|根拠地= {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}
{{ill|Hinokuma Shrine|en|Hinokuma Shrine|qid=Q888184|lt=Hinokuma Shrine and Kunikakasu Shrine|1=Hinokuma Shrine}}<br/>{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}
{{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama, Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}
|人物= [[#人物]]を参照
|支流= Ōda no Hori, Yamashi, Tanba Tatenui-shi, Tsumekou-shi, Takano-shi, {{ill|Ōmura Clan (Divine Clan)|ja|大村氏 (神別)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Clan (Divine Clan)|1=Ōmura Clan (Shinbetsu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11436025}}, {{ill|Fujitsu Tachikuni no Mikoto|ja|藤津立国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujitsu Tachikuni no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwami no Kuni no Mikoto|ja|石見国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami no Kuni no Mikoto|1=Iwami Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60990902}}, Niu no Hori, Narahara-shi, Osakake-shi, {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|qid=Q8189229|lt=Imibe-shi|1=Inbe clan}}, Kannu-shi, Niu Aimi-shi
}}

'''Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (紀伊国造) refers to the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} who governed {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} (modern {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}). This clan of Miyatsuko descended from the {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}, specifically the '''Ki-shi''' (紀氏). Historically, they held the dual responsibilities of the office of Miyatsuko in Kii Province and the priestly duties of {{ill|Hinokuma Shrine|en|Hinokuma Shrine|qid=Q888184|lt=Hinokuma Shrine and Kunikakasu Shrine|1=Hinokuma Shrine}}. Even after the abolition of the Miyatsuko system during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, they retained the title "Miyatsuko" as hereditary priests (宮司) of these shrines.

== Overview ==

=== Name Variants ===
In the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, the name is recorded as 紀伊国造 (Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko). Older texts use variants such as '''Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (木国造) in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and '''Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (紀国造) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.

=== Ancestry ===
* The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'s "Miyatsuko Chronicle" attributes the establishment of Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko to {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. The {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} concurs, stating that Ame no Michine no Mikoto was granted control of Kii Province after Emperor Jimmu’s unification of the Kinai region.
* The first historically verifiable Miyatsuko is likely {{ill|Ki no Oshikatsu|ja|紀忍勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Oshikatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀押勝|1=ki no oshikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q58139743}}, mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} (entries for Emperor Bidatsu's 12th year, 7th and 10th months).<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=古代氏族の系譜を読み解く|date=2022nd year 2月1日|publisher=吉川弘文館|page=134|author=鈴木正信}}</ref>

=== Clan Lineage ===
The Ki clan (紀氏) originally held the {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}} title and later transitioned to {{ill|Natori|ja|直 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natori|1=Atai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55526687}} in the {{ill|Kōfu Nenki|ja|庚午年籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Nenki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=Koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11410913}}. By 835 CE, they received the {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|qid=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}} title.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}「第1章 天神系氏族　第6節 紀国造氏族　1紀国造紀直、紀宿祢（一）」『古代氏族系譜集成』中巻、古代氏族研究会、879-881頁。</ref>

The Ki clan served as priests of Hinokuma and Kunikakasu shrines for over two millennia. The preservation of such a lineage, aside from the Imperial Family, is rare. Comparable examples include the {{ill|Izumo Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineages of the Senge and Kitajima families and priestly clans such as the {{ill|Aso Shrine|en|Aso Shrine|qid=Q715639|lt=Aso Shrine|1=Aso Shrine}}'s Aso family or the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|qid=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}'s Tsumori family.<ref>『[[Jōgan Gishiki|貞観儀式]]』巻第10。</ref>

== Historical Evolution ==
The Miyatsuko family adapted through Japan’s tumultuous history. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, they maintained regional influence through alliances with the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|qid=Q1069187|lt=Kii Domain|1=Kishū Domain}} and others. Despite the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}’s challenges, including the {{ill|Siege of Kii Province|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Kii Province|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855721}}, the clan continued to serve as priests and feudal administrators.

=== Transition to Modern Era ===
In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the family entered the {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|qid=Q241734|lt=Kazoku|1=Kazoku}} peerage system, obtaining baronial status. Members served in government roles, such as {{ill|Baron Ki Toshihide|ja|紀俊秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baron Ki Toshihide|1=Toshihide Kii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604973}}, who represented the family in the {{ill|Chamber of Peers|en|Chamber of Peers|qid=Q2948408|lt=House of Peers|1=Chamber of Peers}}.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|date=2006th year （平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=小田部雄次|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[中央公論新社]]|series=[[中公新書]]1836|isbn= 978-4121018366|ref=harv}}</ref>

== Lineage of Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko ==
{{columns-list|colwidth=25em|
# {{ill|Ame no Michine no Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Michine no Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940767}}
# {{ill|Hiko Makoto no Mikoto|ja|比古麻命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Makoto no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
# {{ill|Onitore no Mikoto|ja|鬼刀禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onitore no Mikoto|1=Kitone-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97636304}}
...
# {{ill|Ki Toshihide|ja|紀俊秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Toshihide|1=Toshihide Kii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604973}} - {{ill|Mayor of Wakayama|ja|和歌山市長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mayor of Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和歌山市|1=Wakayama (city)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q200747}}, {{ill|House of Peers (Japan)|ja|貴族院議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院|1=Chamber of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2948408}}
...
}}
== Genealogy ==

* The "Ki Clan Genealogy" recorded in {{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|qid=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku|1=Sonpi Bunmyaku}} and {{ill|Gunsho Ruijū|en|Gunsho Ruijū|qid=Q1554852|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} is inconsistent across generations and cannot be trusted as it stands. Here, , {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ki Clan Genealogical Records|ja|紀氏家牒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan Genealogical Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}}, although it is noted that there are still differing opinions regarding the early sections and the positions of individuals such as {{ill|Ki Maji|ja|紀麻路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Maji|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605129}}, {{ill|Ki Sarutori|ja|紀猿取|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Sarutori|1=Ki no Sarutori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q98083047}}, {{ill|Ki Tanaga|ja|紀田長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Tanaga|1=Ki no Tanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q106196670}}, and {{ill|Ki no Haseo|en|Ki no Haseo|qid=Q118767|lt=Ki Haseo|1=Ki no Haseo}}.

{{familytree/start|style=font-size:80%}}

{{familytree |border=0| |)|-|-|v|-|-|.|||!}}

{{ill|Ki District|ja|紀伊郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki District|1=Kii district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604966}}, etc.
|人物 = {{ill|Ki no Oyu|ja|紀小弓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Oyu|1=Ki no Koyumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605024}}<br/>{{ill|Ki no Oiwashi|ja|紀大磐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Oiwashi|1=Ki no Ōiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605012}}<br/>{{ill|Ki no Kosami|ja|紀古佐美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kosami|1=Ki no Kosami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3825109}}<br/>{{ill|Ki no Haseo|en|Ki no Haseo|qid=Q118767|lt=Ki no Haseo|1=Ki no Haseo}}<br/>{{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|qid=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}}
|後裔 = {{ill|Tsuno District, Yamaguchi|en|Tsuno District, Yamaguchi|qid=Q7850232|lt=Tsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Tsuno District, Yamaguchi}}<br/>{{ill|Sakamoto Clan|ja|坂本氏|lt=Sakamoto Asomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<br/>{{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|qid=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda Clan|1=Ikeda clan}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|qid=Q2927809}})<br/>{{ill|Mashiko Clan|ja|益子氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Clan|1=Q11581185|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581185}} (Samurai)<br/>{{ill|Hotta clan|en|Hotta clan|qid=Q751004|lt=Hotta Clan|1=Hotta clan}} ({{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}})<br/>{{ill|Tanaka Clan|ja|田中家 (紀氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shrine Family)<br/>{{ill|Zenpoji Clan|ja|善法寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpoji Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shrine Family)<br/>{{ill|Zaizen Clan|ja|財前氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaizen Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Samurai), etc.
}}

'''Kinokuno Clan''' (紀氏, ''Ki no Uji'') is a clan whose family name is "Ki."

The Kinokuno Clan was an ancient {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|qid=Q84066|lt=Gōzoku|1=Gōzoku}} based in {{ill|Kinori|ja|紀里|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Heguri District, Yamato Province (present-day Kamisho, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri|1=Heguri, Nara}}, Ikoma District, Nara Prefecture). Initially holding the title of {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}, they were renamed {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}} under the {{ill|Yatsuka no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsuka no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} system established in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}'s reign (684 AD).

== Overview ==

According to sources like the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Kinokuno Clan descended from {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, with {{ill|Ki no Tsuno|ja|紀角|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Tsuno|1=Ki no Tsuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605107}}, a son of {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}, as their progenitor. Both Ki no Tsuno and his predecessor were maternally descended from the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} family. This relationship suggests that the clan served the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} early on as a military house. Figures like {{ill|Ki no Oyu|ja|紀小弓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Oyu|1=Ki no Koyumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605024}}, {{ill|Ki no Oiwashi|ja|紀大磐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Oiwashi|1=Ki no Ōiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605012}}, and {{ill|Ki no Otomaro|ja|紀男麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Otomaro|1=Ki no Omamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605087}} were active in military and diplomatic affairs, particularly regarding the Korean Peninsula, during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzo|1=Emperor Kenzō}}, and {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}.

Although the Kinokuno Clan belonged to the Takenouchi lineage, like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|qid=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi Clan|1=Katsuragi clan}}, {{ill|Kose Clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Clan|1=Kose clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479778}}, and {{ill|Heguri Clan|ja|平群氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri Clan|1=Heguri Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483498}}, they notably did not produce a {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Ōomi|1=Minister (government)}} before the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|qid=Q570140|lt=Taika Reform|1=Taika Reform}} period.

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}, {{ill|Ki no Ōtoko|ja|紀大人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Ōtoko|qq=}} served as the {{ill|Ōtsukasa|ja|御史大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsukasa|1=yushi dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11071155}}. In the Nara period, {{ill|Ki no Maro|ja|紀麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Maro|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605127}} became a {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}, {{ill|Ki no Masaru|ja|紀麻路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Masaru|1=Ki no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605129}} a {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}}, and {{ill|Ki no Iimaro|ja|紀飯麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Iimaro|1=Ki no Iimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605128}} a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}. The clan flourished as an {{ill|Consort kin|en|Consort kin|qid=Q1050320|lt=Outer Clan|1=Consort kin}} after {{ill|Prince Shirakabe|ja|白壁王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shirakabe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 光仁天皇|1=Emperor Kōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q958662}}, born to {{ill|Ki no Tsuji Hime|ja|紀橡姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Tsuji Hime|1=Ki no Tochihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24868439}} and {{ill|Prince Shiki|en|Prince Shiki|qid=Q11072254|lt=Prince Shiki|1=Prince Shiki}}, ascended as {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|qid=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin|1=Emperor Kōnin}}. However, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the clan's influence waned due to the rise of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|qid=Q2975268|lt=Fujiwara Hokke|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}}.

By the early Heian period, only a few members, such as {{ill|Ki no Haseo|en|Ki no Haseo|qid=Q118767|lt=Ki no Haseo|1=Ki no Haseo}}, managed to hold significant positions. They later transitioned to literary contributions, producing figures like {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|qid=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}}, {{ill|Ki no Tomonori|en|Ki no Tomonori|qid=Q1092686|lt=Ki no Tomonori|1=Ki no Tomonori}}, and {{ill|Ki no Tokibumi|en|Ki no Tokibumi|qid=Q3196291|lt=Ki no Tokifumi|1=Ki no Tokibumi}}.

== References ==
